summary: Dependency-confusion package exfiltrates full environment and system info to hardcoded IP on install
details: Package publishes version 999.99.99 to win resolution against an internal `setka-editor` package on private registries. package.json registers callback.js as both preinstall and postinstall hooks, so the payload auto-fires on `npm install`. callback.js collects the installer's username, uid/gid, home directory, hostname, current working directory, local IP, external IP (via https://api.ipify.org), CI flag, and the entire process.env without masking, then POSTs the JSON blob to the hardcoded bare-IP plaintext C2 endpoint http://132.243.20.244:8000/api/collect with `User-Agent: dependency-confusion/setka-editor`. On developer or CI machines this leaks any secrets present in environment variables (npm tokens, GitHub tokens, AWS keys, CI credentials) to the attacker-controlled host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
